verses.text: David prepares a place for the ark and declares, 'none ought to carry the ark of God but the Levites,' having learned from Uzza's death. This time he searches God's word for the right way. Chastened, he sets out to do the holy work as God commanded.

David prepares a place for the ark and declares, 'none ought to carry the ark of God but the Levites,' having learned from Uzza's death. This time he searches God's word for the right way. Chastened, he sets out to do the holy work as God commanded.

David prepares a place for the ark and declares, 'none ought to carry the ark of God but the Levites,' having learned from Uzza's death. This time he searches God's word for the right way. Chastened, he sets out to do the holy work as God commanded.
David gathers the Levite families to bear the ark. Each house is summoned to its appointed part. The work of God is done in order, with every servant called to his proper place.

David gathers the Levite families to bear the ark. Each house is summoned to its appointed part. The work of God is done in order, with every servant called to his proper place.
More Levite chiefs and their brethren are assembled for the task. The muster is careful and complete. When God's work is done His way, it is done with reverence and proper preparation.
More Levite chiefs and their brethren are assembled for the task. The muster is careful and complete. When God's work is done His way, it is done with reverence and proper preparation.

David charges the priests and Levites to sanctify themselves, confessing the LORD broke out before 'because we sought him not after the due order'; now they bear the ark on their shoulders as Moses commanded. Holiness and obedience, not zeal alone, please God.

David appoints the chief Levites to set their brethren as singers with instruments, 'lifting up the voice with joy.' The ministry of music is organized for the LORD's house. Joyful, ordered praise is given a central, honoured place in worship.

Singers, players, and trumpeting priests are arranged, with Chenaniah set over the song because he was skilful. Excellence is sought in the service of praise. God deserves trained skill, not careless effort, in the worship His people offer.

The ark is brought up with sacrifices and great joy, David dancing before the LORD in a linen ephod like a Levite. The king lays aside his dignity to worship. True joy in God is not too proud to humble itself before Him.

The ark is brought up with sacrifices and great joy, David dancing before the LORD in a linen ephod like a Levite. The king lays aside his dignity to worship. True joy in God is not too proud to humble itself before Him.

Michal watches from a window and despises David in her heart for his undignified joy. Where all Israel rejoiced, she sneered. A cold, proud heart scorns the very abandon of worship that God delights to see.
